name: West Virginia Occupational Therapist License
description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to West Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: West Virginia Board of Occupational Therapy

address.description:
West Virginia Board of Occupational Therapy
110 MacCorkle Ave. SE
Charleston, WV 25301

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: NBCOT Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a West Virginia OT license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $100. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.
Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

mainEntity.name: Does West Virginia have reciprocity for occupational therapists?

acceptedAnswer.text: West Virginia has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to West Virginia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in West Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
